headline: Flow matching VLAs now dominate 4/5 LIBERO suites

insights.title: Action head paradigm shift
insights.body: pi0.5 and HybridVLA both use flow matching action heads. Among the top 5 models on LIBERO, 3 now use flow matching or diffusion, up from 1/5 six months ago. Autoregressive tokenization (OpenVLA's approach) is losing ground except in long-horizon tasks.

insights.title: Long-horizon remains unsolved
insights.body: LIBERO-Long scores still lag behind other suites by 15-25%p across all models. Interestingly, CoT-VLA's chain-of-thought reasoning shows the smallest gap (17.1%p) vs pi0.5 (23.4%p gap), suggesting explicit reasoning may help in sequential decision-making.

insights.title: Open-source catching up
insights.body: OpenVLA-OFT (open-source) now trails pi0.5 (closed weights) by only 1.5%p on average LIBERO score. Six months ago, the gap was 6.8%p. LoRA fine-tuning on domain-specific data is proving highly effective.
